summary: First testing pass on VidVault MVP (Expo/React Native + FastAPI). Backend /api/analyze fully verified with 7/7 pytest tests passing (root, supported MP4, unsupported HTML, missing/empty URL, unreachable host, bad scheme). Frontend Home, Analyze (both supported MP4 and unsupported HTML), Downloads, Library, Settings and Player screens all render and interactive elements are reachable. CRITICAL web-preview bug: tapping the Download CTA on the Analyze screen throws 'expo-file-system.getInfoAsync is not available on web...' which flips the Analyze screen into the generic 'Couldn't analyze this URL' error state; this is expected on web (expo-file-system is native-only) but should be handled gracefully so testers/users on web don't think Analyze itself broke. Native (Expo Go/device) download flow was not exercised.

ui_bugs.issue: startDownload() calls expo-file-system.getInfoAsync which is not implemented on web; the thrown error is caught in the analyze screen and reused as message for the generic 'Couldn't analyze this URL' error state, making it look like analyze itself failed. Should either (a) detect Platform.OS === 'web' and show a friendly 'Downloading is only available in the mobile app' message, or (b) route the error to a toast/alert without wiping the successful analyze result.

tabBarTestID values (tab-home, tab-downloads, tab-library, tab-settings) do not surface as data-testid attributes on the actual DOM element on web (react-navigation places them on inner containers). Tab clicks by testID selector are therefore unreliable in web-based Playwright tests. Consider wrapping tab labels in a Pressable with an explicit testID or using tabBarButton for testable elements.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot verify end-to-end on web preview because startDownload fails immediately with expo-file-system error. Needs a device/Expo Go run to confirm progress row + progress bar appear in Downloads tab.

react_native_issues.component: React Native Web shadow* / pointerEvents props
react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow"' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Likely coming from custom Card / PrimaryButton components. Migrate shadow* to boxShadow and move pointerEvents into style.

action_items: In app/analyze.tsx onDownload catch: detect Platform.OS === 'web' (or check for the expo-file-system web error) and show a non-destructive toast/inline message instead of setting state to 'error', so the analyzed video info remains visible.
Optionally in src/services/downloader.ts short-circuit on web with a clear thrown Error like 'Downloads are only supported in the mobile app'.
Migrate deprecated shadow* style props to boxShadow and pointerEvents prop to style.pointerEvents (likely in src/components/Card.tsx and PrimaryButton.tsx).
Consider replacing tabBarTestID with tabBarButton wrapper carrying an explicit testID so web/Playwright tests can reach tabs reliably.
Native device retest recommended for the full download lifecycle (start -> progress -> pause/resume -> complete -> play in built-in player).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py analyze() correctly falls back HEAD -> Range GET and returns supported=false with a helpful reason for HTML pages; 400 for empty url and non-http(s); httpx errors caught and returned as supported=false. All good.

Backend imports motor/Mongo even though MVP does not persist anything server-side — acceptable placeholder, but the module will crash at import time if MONGO_URL/DB_NAME are missing. Guarded by env access via os.environ[...] which is correct per project convention.
Frontend uses process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL correctly (verified via analyzer.ts through UI). CORS wide open — fine for MVP.
app/player.tsx uses expo-video (not deprecated expo-av) — good.
app/analyze.tsx propagates any startDownload exception into the analyze screen's error state, wiping the video card. Should be isolated.
No hardcoded URLs; all network calls use env var.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (all screens render, all interactive elements reachable, download start blocked on web only)

seed_data_creation: None. History/favorites/downloads are AsyncStorage-backed and start empty; verified empty states.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend base URL: process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (video-fetch-74.preview.emergentagent.com). Backend suite: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_analyze_api.py -v. Known-good direct MP4: https://download.blender.org/peach/bigbuckbunny_movies/BigBuckBunny_320x180.mp4. Known-bad HTML: https://www.example.com/. Tabs are not selectable via data-testid on web — navigate by URL (/downloads, /library, /settings, /analyze?url=..., /player?uri=...). The download start step fails on the web preview because expo-file-system has no web implementation; retest download progression on Expo Go / native device.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of 'Couldn't analyze this URL: The method or property expo-file-system.getInfoAsync is not available on web': (1) User taps Download on the analyze screen after a successful analyze; (2) startDownload() in src/services/downloader.ts calls FileSystem.getInfoAsync (from expo-file-system/legacy or expo-file-system) which throws on web; (3) analyze.tsx onDownload's catch sets state to {kind:'error', message}, replacing the previously rendered supported video card with the generic error screen. Mitigation: (a) short-circuit startDownload on Platform.OS === 'web' with a friendly Error, and (b) in onDownload's catch use a non-destructive UI signal (Toast/Alert) instead of setState error so the analyze result is preserved.
